name: mobilenet_v3

methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, variant: str = 'large', width_mult: float = 1.0, dropout: float = 0.2)

`variant`    — "large" or "small".
`width_mult` — uniform channel multiplier.
`dropout`    — classifier dropout probability.

members.name: MobileNetV3

methods.name: __init__
methods.summary: Initialise the ReLU module. See the class docstring for parameter semantics.

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Return bias tensor of shape (1, num_heads, ws², ws²).

members.summary: MobileNet v3 feature-extracting backbone (no classification head).
members.extended:
Implements the NAS-designed topology from Howard et al.,
"Searching for MobileNetV3", ICCV 2019 ([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)).  The
architecture combines MobileNet-v2's inverted-residual block
with three new ingredients selected by a platform-aware
architecture search: a lightweight squeeze-and-excitation
module in selected blocks, the **hard-swish** activation
($x \cdot \mathrm{ReLU6}(x+3) / 6$) in the deeper half
of the network, and a redesigned head that moves the expensive
1×1 expansion *after* the global average pool.

Two hand-tuned variants are exposed: `"large"` (15 blocks,
targeting higher-accuracy mobile deployments) and `"small"`
(11 blocks, targeting tight latency budgets).  Channel counts
scale uniformly with `config.width_mult`.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the factory functions
(`mobilenet_v3_large`, `mobilenet_v3_small`) for
paper-cited variants.

examples:
Build a MobileNet-v3-Large backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_large
>>> backbone = mobilenet_v3_large()
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = backbone(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(2, 960, 1, 1)
notes:
The hard-swish activation,



$$
\mathrm{h\text{-}swish}(x) = x \cdot \frac{\mathrm{ReLU6}(x + 3)}{6},
$$

closely approximates swish/SiLU using only piecewise-linear
primitives, making it dramatically cheaper to evaluate on
mobile-friendly fixed-point hardware.  Combined with the
redesigned tail (moving the 1×1 expansion to operate on a
single spatial location after global average pooling), the
overall network achieves $3.2\%$ higher ImageNet top-1
accuracy than MobileNet-v2 at $20\%$ lower latency on a
Pixel-1 CPU.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: features
attributes.description:
Stem + 11 (Small) or 15 (Large) inverted-residual SE blocks
+ 1×1 penultimate expansion.  Channel counts scale with
`config.width_mult`.

attributes.name: avgpool
attributes.description:
Global average pool collapsing the final feature map to
`(B, C, 1, 1)`.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Per-stage descriptor (channels + reduction factor) exposed
via `BackboneMixin` for downstream FPN / decoder
modules.

members.name: MobileNetV3ForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)

members.summary: MobileNet v3 image classifier with the redesigned inverted head.
members.extended:
Combines a `MobileNetV3` feature stack with the paper's
*redesigned* classification head, which is the central
efficiency innovation of v3.  Instead of the conventional
`GAP → Linear` head, v3 places a $1 \times 1$
convolution *after* the global average pool — when the spatial
map has already collapsed to `1 × 1` — saving roughly
$10\%$ of total latency compared to v2's pre-pool
expansion.  The full head is

$$
\operatorname{AvgPool} \to \operatorname{Conv}_{1\times1}
    \to \mathrm{h\text{-}swish} \to \operatorname{Flatten}
    \to \operatorname{Dropout} \to \operatorname{Linear}.
$$

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture spec.  Use the `*_cls` factory functions
(`mobilenet_v3_large_cls`, `mobilenet_v3_small_cls`)
for paper-cited variants.

examples:
Run inference on a batch of 224×224 RGB images:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_large_cls
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large_cls()
>>> x = lucid.randn(4, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(4, 1000)
Build the smaller variant for latency-critical deployment:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_small_cls
>>> small = mobilenet_v3_small_cls(num_classes=10)
>>> small.config.variant
'small'
notes:
When `labels` are supplied to `forward`, a categorical
cross-entropy loss is computed against the logits and returned
alongside them.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: features
attributes.description: Same NAS-designed feature stack as on `MobileNetV3`.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
The redesigned head described above, projecting from the
penultimate channel count (960 for Large, 576 for Small) to
`config.num_classes` via a 1280-ch (Large) or 1024-ch
(Small) intermediate width.

members.name: mobilenet_v3_large
members.extended:
Builds a `MobileNetV3` with the NAS-designed Large
topology from Howard et al., 2019 (Table 1): 15 inverted-residual
bottleneck blocks with selective SE attention and hard-swish in
the deeper half, followed by a 1×1 expansion to 960 channels.
Approximately 5.4M parameters and 75.2% ImageNet-1k top-1
accuracy (Table 3) — the higher-accuracy variant of the family.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored — the returned model is randomly initialised.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `MobileNetV3Config`
(e.g. `width_mult=0.75` for a slimmer variant).

returns.description:
Backbone with the MobileNet-v3-Large configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_large
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 960, 1, 1)
notes:
See Howard et al., "Searching for MobileNetV3", ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)), Table 1 (V3-Large spec) and Table 3
(ImageNet accuracy).

members.name: mobilenet_v3_large_cls
members.signature: mobilenet_v3_large_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: MobileNetV3LargeWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: MobileNet-v3-Large image classifier with the redesigned head.
members.extended:
Builds a `MobileNetV3ForImageClassification` with the
Large topology (Howard et al., 2019) plus the redesigned
inverted classification head (`GAP → 1×1 Conv → h-swish →
Flatten → Dropout → Linear`).  Approximately 5.4M parameters
and 75.2% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy (Table 3).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag
(`MobileNetV3LargeWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a tag string →
that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with `weights`
(which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `MobileNetV3Config`
(typically `num_classes` to retarget the classifier).

returns.description:
Classifier with the MobileNet-v3-Large configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it), optionally
initialised from pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_large_cls
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large_cls(num_classes=10)
>>> x = lucid.randn(2, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(2, 10)
Load ImageNet-pretrained weights:
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> from lucid.models.weights import MobileNetV3LargeWeights
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_large_cls(weights=MobileNetV3LargeWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)
notes:
See Howard et al., "Searching for MobileNetV3", ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)), Table 1 and Table 3.  Pretrained weights are
converted from torchvision's `MobileNet_V3_Large_Weights` and
hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under `lucid-dl/mobilenet-v3-large`.

members.name: mobilenet_v3_small
members.extended:
Builds a `MobileNetV3` with the NAS-designed Small
topology from Howard et al., 2019 (Table 2): 11 inverted-residual
bottleneck blocks with selective SE attention and aggressive
hard-swish usage from the very first stage, followed by a 1×1
expansion to 576 channels.  Approximately 2.9M parameters and
67.4% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy (Table 3) — the lower-latency
variant of the family, targeting tight mobile budgets.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently
ignored.

returns.description:
Backbone with the MobileNet-v3-Small configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_small
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_small()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.last_hidden_state.shape
(1, 576, 1, 1)
notes:
See Howard et al., "Searching for MobileNetV3", ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)), Table 2 (V3-Small spec) and Table 3
(ImageNet accuracy).

members.name: mobilenet_v3_small_cls
members.signature: mobilenet_v3_small_cls(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: MobileNetV3SmallWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: MobileNet-v3-Small image classifier with the redesigned head.
members.extended:
Builds a `MobileNetV3ForImageClassification` with the
Small topology (Howard et al., 2019) plus the redesigned
inverted classification head.  Approximately 2.9M parameters
and 67.4% ImageNet-1k top-1 accuracy (Table 3) — the
latency-focused variant of the family.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True`
→ the `DEFAULT` tag
(`MobileNetV3SmallWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1`); a tag string →
that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with `weights`
(which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over
`pretrained`.

returns.description:
Classifier with the MobileNet-v3-Small configuration applied
(or with `overrides` merged on top of it), optionally
initialised from pretrained weights.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mobilenet_v3 import mobilenet_v3_small_cls
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_small_cls()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
Load ImageNet-pretrained weights:
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_small_cls(pretrained=True)
>>> from lucid.models.weights import MobileNetV3SmallWeights
>>> model = mobilenet_v3_small_cls(weights=MobileNetV3SmallWeights.IMAGENET1K_V1)
notes:
See Howard et al., "Searching for MobileNetV3", ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)), Table 2 and Table 3.  Pretrained weights are
converted from torchvision's `MobileNet_V3_Small_Weights` and
hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under `lucid-dl/mobilenet-v3-small`.

members.name: MobileNetV3LargeWeights
members.extended: Howard et al. ICCV 2019 Large (5.5 M params, top-1 74.04%).
notes:
Reference: Howard et al., *"Searching for MobileNetV3"*, ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 74.042% / top-5 91.340%),
sourced from
`torchvision/MobileNet_V3_Large_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

members.name: MobileNetV3SmallWeights
members.extended: Howard et al. ICCV 2019 Small (2.5 M params, top-1 67.67%).
notes:
Reference: Howard et al., *"Searching for MobileNetV3"*, ICCV 2019
([arXiv:1905.02244](https://arxiv.org/abs/1905.02244)).

attributes.name: IMAGENET1K_V1
attributes.description:
ImageNet-1k V1 checkpoint (top-1 67.668% / top-5 87.402%),
sourced from
`torchvision/MobileNet_V3_Small_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`.

summary: MobileNet v3 family — Howard et al., 2019.
citation: Howard, Andrew, et al. "Searching for MobileNetV3." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision, 2019, pp. 1314–1324.
theory:
MobileNet-v3 is the result of an explicit *neural architecture
search* layered on top of the inverted-residual block of v2.
A platform-aware NAS (MnasNet-style) selects the per-stage
expansion ratio, kernel size, and channel count to minimise
a latency-and-accuracy joint objective on a real mobile CPU,
after which a NetAdapt pass fine-tunes the channel counts of
each layer individually under a hard latency budget.  The
output is two hand-tuned variants — **Large** and **Small** —
targeting different parts of the latency/accuracy frontier.

Two architectural refinements give v3 its accuracy gain over v2.
First, a lightweight **squeeze-and-excitation** module is inserted
inside selected bottleneck blocks; it pools the spatial dimensions
and re-weights the channel responses, providing a cheap
channel-attention mechanism.  Second, a new activation
function called **hard-swish**,



$$
\mathrm{h\text{-}swish}(x) = x \cdot \frac{\mathrm{ReLU6}(x + 3)}{6},
$$

replaces swish/SiLU in the deeper half of the network.  Hard-swish
matches swish closely in shape but uses only piecewise-linear
primitives, making it dramatically cheaper to evaluate on
mobile-friendly fixed-point hardware.

A further trick is rewriting the expensive final layers of v2.
The $1 \times 1$ expansion before the global average pool
is moved *after* the pool, where it operates on a single
spatial location, saving roughly $10\%$ of total latency.
Combined, these changes give MobileNet-v3-Large
$3.2\%$ higher ImageNet top-1 accuracy than v2 at
$20\%$ lower latency on a Pixel-1 CPU.
